titleOptions.en: Record of a Shattered Soul's Questioning of Heaven

synopsis: In the mythic age of the Nine Furnaces, a jade Seal of Heaven's Mandate is stolen from a neutral mountain shrine, and the warring polities of the river-world erupt into a great war over who may rightfully rule. Wu Lie, the magnificent and undefeated lord of the iron city of Yong, swears brotherhood beneath a peach grove with the strategist Zhao Ming and the wild-born champion Jing Tong, whose love becomes the still center of his violent life. But a slight in council, a borrowed suit of armor, and a strategist's cold arithmetic conspire to kill Jing Tong on Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope, and the war-story becomes a grief-story. Unmade by loss, Wu Lie abandons the contest of kingdoms and journeys west to the Dark Metropolis at the edge of the world, where Lady Silence offers him the Water of Undying and tells him its price: that immortality is not escape from grief but its endless, clear-eyed extension, paid for with the lives of those one loves. He refuses, and walks home through a world slowly returning to color, to the declining garden of his house, where the lady Yun Suhua has kept the record of everything that was lost. The Seal is recovered but its cipher has faded; the mandate of the age remains, as ever, a matter of reading. The whole epic proves to be her elegy.

characters.role: The protagonist; magnificent, undefeated lord of the iron city of Yong, part-divine, ungovernable, who after his sworn brother's death journeys to the world's edge to wrest immortality from Heaven and learns its price.
characters.source: Gilgamesh (the mortality-quest hero), with Achilles's martial dominance and wrath

characters.role: Wu Lie's sworn brother, a wild man of the mountain forests civilized into a peerless champion; 'the mirror-companion'. His death at mid-tale is the hinge around which the whole epic turns.
characters.source: Enkidu / Patroclus (the beloved companion-sacrifice), with Guan Yu's loyalty and valor

characters.role: The third sworn brother and master strategist who holds the alliance together by will alone; his battle-calculus sends Jing Tong to his death, a necessity he can neither prevent nor confess, and his guilt shapes the second half.
characters.source: Zhuge Liang's brilliance fused with Cao Cao's cold calculation and Hector's doomed duty

characters.role: A consumptive, clear-seeing lady of the house's garden world who loves Jing Tong without ever naming it, foresees the ruin, and becomes the keeper of the declining house's record; the dreaming consciousness through whom the epic is finally revealed to be told.
characters.source: Lin Daiyu (the garden elegist) from Dream of the Red Chamber; the narrating dream-frame

characters.role: The fog-heir prince of a rival state who steals the Seal, believing with some justice that it was taken from his house generations ago; morally ambiguous, a fine poet and reluctant warrior, he deals the fatal blow at Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope and falls at last to Wu Lie in a duel without triumph.
characters.source: Paris (the transgressor who triggers the war) fused with Hector's honor; the Iliad's moral ambiguity

characters.role: The goddess-ruler of the Dark Metropolis at the world's western edge, keeper of the dead, who receives Wu Lie because he arrives with a genuine question, gives him the Water of Undying, and tells him exactly what it costs.
characters.source: Utnapishtim / Siduri (the immortal who reveals death's truth) from Gilgamesh

outline.en: Brotherhood Sworn in the Peach Grove; the Dark Omen Comes into the World
outline.beats: A famine-year riot in Yong reveals the resentment Wu Lie's magnificence has bought; quelling it, he meets the wild-born Jing Tong and they fight to the first standstill of his life. Under a peach grove, Wu Lie, Jing Tong, and the strategist Zhao Ming swear brotherhood. The sacred jade Seal of Heaven's Mandate and the garden world of the house are introduced; the lady Yun Suhua watches and writes that two flames have met without burning the city — a portent worse than fire.

outline.en: The Pole-Star Loses Its Seal; the Nine Lands Boil Over
outline.beats: Wu Yin steals the Seal from the neutral mountain shrine in a single perfect night. By dawn the priests cry the loss along the river by signal-fire and the realm fractures; two polities submit to the thief's new legitimacy, the rest form a fractious alliance. Wu Lie, certain that a stolen mandate is void, becomes the alliance's war-fist while Zhao Ming designs the campaign. A rhapsodic catalogue of the gathering armies.

outline.en: Autumn Feast in the Vermilion Garden; the Verse-Omens Are Written First
outline.beats: The tale turns wholly into the garden world. Yun Suhua and the women of the house hold a chrysanthemum poetry contest; their seasonal lyrics are coded prophecies of the house's fall. Jing Tong comes once to deliver a message and lingers outside the wall to hear them; he never sees Yun Suhua, but she sees his shadow on the wall. The slowest, most luminous, most quietly devastating chapter.

outline.en: Jing Tong Breaks the Banner; Night-Speech in the Savage Hills
outline.beats: The campaign's first great victory: Jing Tong rides alone into the enemy's best formation and kills their commander before five thousand witnesses. That night, by the fires of the recaptured hills, he asks Wu Lie whether he fears death, and Wu Lie answers, 'I have never met it; why should I fear a stranger?' — words that will haunt all that follows. The brothers compose regulated verse together; Yun Suhua, a field-scribe, notes that death has met him, and is patient.

outline.en: The Loyal Champion Is Slandered; In Wrath He Lies in an Empty Tent
outline.beats: After a victory that embarrasses the high command, the alliance's factions strip Wu Lie's house of its honors and reassign its credit. His wrath is cold and absolute; he withdraws his arms from the field and burns every emissary's letter. Without him the alliance bleeds; Zhao Ming's finest plans cannot be executed by lesser hands. Jing Tong sits outside Wu Lie's tent through the nights, unable to watch the cause fail.

outline.en: Wearing the Borrowed Armor into the Lines; the Fall at Ten-Thousand-Spirit Slope
outline.beats: The catastrophe. Jing Tong, unable to bear the defeats, dons Wu Lie's armor and standard and leads the house's troops himself. Seeing the standard, Wu Yin commits everything; Zhao Ming's arithmetic required the western approach to be held only briefly, and it is — Jing Tong holds four minutes past the calculation and dies surrounded by the enemy's dead. The chapter ends in a long-lined lament in the manner of the Li Sao: where does the mirror-companion go, whose reflection I was?

outline.en: Demanding an Accounting by Lamplight; Zhao Ming Holds Back His Tears
outline.beats: Wu Lie learns that Zhao Ming knew the approach would fall. Zhao Ming does not lie; he lays out the arithmetic, the ten thousand who came home because one man did not. Wu Lie listens, then asks: 'The ten thousand — do any of them know Jing Tong's name?' He does not dissolve the brotherhood; he simply goes silent, and his silence becomes a country no one can leave. A truce is struck, the Seal still lost, and Wu Lie begins to ask after the roads to the western edge of the world.

outline.en: Travelling West to Question the Dead; the River of Forgetting Grants a Road
outline.beats: Wu Lie's descent. He travels alone through landscapes that depart from the real — a plain of grass that repeats the last words of the dead, a city where the dead queue for a judgment that never comes, a mountain that is a painting by day. The old River-God grants him passage only after he gives, at last, one true and dishonest answer: that he fears nothing. He passes into the Dark Metropolis, a vast dim bureaucratic city of the recently dead; Jing Tong has moved on to the further archive.

outline.en: The Water of Undying; Lady Silence Shows Its Price
outline.beats: Lady Silence receives Wu Lie because he comes with a question, not a demand, and leads him to the Water of Undying. She explains its nature before he drinks: it is not immortality but a loan, drawn invisibly from the remaining years of those one loves, and a clarity of remembered grief that never ends. He sits beside the water three days, thinks of Zhao Ming and Yun Suhua, and does not drink. He asks whether anything is truly free; she answers: memory — it costs the rememberer, not the remembered.

outline.en: The Vermilion Garden Falls; the Elegy, a Single Dream
outline.beats: The long walk home, the world returning to color. Wu Lie finds his house in its first decline — debts, sons who did not return, a failing patriarch — and the garden still standing. The Seal is recovered in a ceremony that should be triumphant and is only strange: its cipher has faded almost past reading. He sits with Yun Suhua beneath the persimmon tree and says, 'Tell me everything that happened while I was gone.' It is revealed that the whole epic has been her record, her elegy; a terminal lament in all the voices, living and dead, closes the book.
